title: Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity
subtitle: Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.
caption:
Employment discrimination based on sexual orientation or gender identity

Includes discrimination in hiring, promotion, termination and harassment at work. Only legal status considered, not enforcement.

This choropleth world map displays the legal status of employment discrimination protections based on sexual orientation or gender identity for the year 2025. The legend categorizes countries into seven distinct groups: protections for both sexual orientation and gender identity (dark blue), gender identity only (green), sexual orientation only (light yellow), varies by region (orange), ambiguous status (red-orange), no protections (red), and no data (grey hatching). A large portion of the map is colored red, indicating no protections; this includes the entirety of Russia, China, and the African continent, with the exception of South Africa and Namibia which appear in blue. Dark blue, representing comprehensive protections for both categories, covers the United States, Canada, Brazil, Argentina, Australia, New Zealand, and most of Western Europe. Green areas, signifying protections for gender identity only, are visible in India and parts of South America such as Chile. Light yellow regions, indicating sexual orientation protections only, appear in Mexico and scattered across parts of Africa and Asia. The "varies by region" category, shown in orange, applies to countries like Japan and Malaysia. Several nations in the Middle East and Central Asia are colored red-orange, denoting an ambiguous legal status. Greenland and Antarctica are marked with grey hatching to indicate no data. The visual distribution shows a concentration of comprehensive legal protections in the Americas and Oceania, while vast regions of Africa, Asia, and Eastern Europe fall into the no protections category.
